title: Size effects on generation-recombination noise
verified_location: Eqs. 1-3, drift-diffusion/Poisson equations, terminal current, Eqs. 20-21
terminal_boundary_condition: voltage-biased current noise; conduction plus displacement current

limitations: not bipolar; ideal contacts; not HgCdTe-specific

title: Generation-recombination noise modelling in semiconductor devices through population or approximate equivalent current density fluctuations
verified_location: Abstract; Introduction; Secs. 2-4; conclusion; exact coefficient transcription pending
physical_model: fundamental population versus equivalent current sources
recombination_model: direct and SRH with trap occupation
covariance_basis: fundamental population Langevin sources proportional to forward plus reverse transition rates; explicit electron-hole-trap coupling
psd_convention: frequency-domain voltage PSD; exact one/two-sided normalization pending
main_assumptions: finite uniformly doped sample; ohmic contacts; bipolar drift-diffusion; direct or SRH transitions

title: Effects of blocking contacts on generation-recombination noise and responsivity in intrinsic photoconductors

material: includes x approximately 0.2 HgCdTe at 77 K
contact_boundary_conditions: contact recombination velocity from ohmic to blocking
main_result: blocking contacts alter responsivity and GR-noise rolloff

title: Theory of deep level trap effects on generation-recombination noise in HgCdTe photoconductors
authors: A. Evan Iverson; D. L. Smith
verified_location: Eq. 1; Eqs. 10a-f; Eqs. 11a-b; Eq. 15; Figs. 1-7; Appendix partially audited
transport_model: quasineutral ambipolar drift-diffusion with trap-modified mobility and diffusivity
electrostatics: quasineutrality imposed; Poisson equation not solved
recombination_model: band-to-band GR plus explicit two-charge-state deep-level capture/emission
contact_boundary_conditions: ohmic end contacts; exact fluctuation conditions pending transcription
terminal_boundary_condition: voltage-noise and responsivity calculation under applied field
noise_primitives: band-to-band thermal generation; background photon generation; bound-free trap exchange
covariance_basis: explicit coupled free-carrier and trap-population fluctuations; exact normalization pending
psd_convention: frequency-domain voltage noise; transform Eq. 15 uses exp(-i omega t)
main_assumptions: 1D length 2L; strong screening/quasineutrality; low-level excitation; uniform n-type HgCdTe
main_result: deep levels change lifetime and sweepout and add a separate trap thermal-noise source
limitations: no self-consistent Poisson; ohmic contacts; selected x=0.21 material and trap parameters
novelty_implication: dynamic trap population and trap thermal noise in HgCdTe are established
